briefdescription.para: The following constants map to the XPAR parameters created in the xparameters.h file.

detaileddescription.para: They are defined here such that a user can easily change all the needed parameters in one place.

memberdef.name: AVB_PTP_RX_INTERRUPT_ID
memberdef.initializer: XPAR_INTC_0_AXIETHERNET_0_AV_INTERRUPT_PTP_RX_VEC_ID /* * AVB PTP Received * Frame Interrupt ID */

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the AVB design.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the instance of the AVB component which is going to be connected to the interrupt controller.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure..

briefdescription.para: This function configures Axi Ethernet core and AVB module.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet driver instance
is a pointer to the AVB instance.

simplesect.para: -XST_SUCCESS to indicate success -XST_FAILURE to indicate failure.

briefdescription.para: This function is the handler which will be called if the PTP drivers identify a possible discontinuity in GrandMaster time.

detaileddescription.para: This handler provides an example of how to handle this situation - but this function is application specific.

parameterdescription.para: contains a callback reference from the driver, in this case it is the instance pointer for the AVB driver.
- a value of 1 indicates that there is a possible discontinuity in GrandMaster time. A value of 0 indicates that Timestamps are no longer uncertain.

simplesect.para: This Handler need to be defined otherwise the XAvb_StubHandler will generate an error

briefdescription.para: This function configures the GMII interface and Axi Ethernet registers for 1000 Mbps speed configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet driver instance
is a pointer to the AVB instance.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure...

briefdescription.para: This function sets the PHY to loopback mode.

detaileddescription.para: This works with the marvell PHY common on Xilinx evaluation boards. This sets the PHY speed to 1000 Mbps.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the AxiEthernet component.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if successful.
XST_FAILURE, in case of failure..

briefdescription.para: For Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the PTP Timer interrupt and PTP Rx interrupt in the INTC module.
This function is called by example code to display a console message.

parameterdescription.para: is the text explaining the error

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for the AVB example.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS to indicate success.
XST_FAILURE to indicate failure
This example will be in a infinite loop if the HW is not working properly and if the interrupts are not received.

briefdescription.para: This file implements a simple example to show the usage of Audio Video Bridging (AVB) functionality of Axi Ethernet IP in loopback mode.

detaileddescription.para: The example uses the PTP Timer and the PTP Rx interrupts. A PDelay_Req packet is sent and is received back (as we are in loopback mode). . After this loop backed PDelay_Req packet is received, a PDelay_Resp and PDelay_RespFollowUp packets are sent. These packets are also received. Since the source port identity of the received packets matches with our systems's own source port identity there is no further processing done.
This code assumes the processor type is Microblaze, Xilinx interrupt controller (XIntc) is used in the system , and that no operating system is used.
It also assumes that all the relevant AVB interrupts are properly connected to the Intc module.
The Ethernet AVB Endpoint functionality should be enabled in the Xilinx Axi Ethernet core for this example to work.
The Axi Ethernet is used with a GMII interface. The example initializes the GMII interface with 1000 Mbps speed.

IMPORTANT NOTE: The user must define the macro XAVB_CLOCK_LOCK_THRESHOLD in xavb.h to an appropriate value as relevant for the corresponding use case. Presently it is defined to 1000 ns which is typical for telecom industry. This macro is used to compare against the slave error as calculated every time after receiving 2 successive sync/followup frames. Slave error is the difference between master time duration and slave time duration as calculated for the time gap (the time it takes to receive two successive sync/follow up frames). If slave error is greater than the value defined in XAVB_CLOCK_LOCK_THRESHOLD, then master and slave clocks are unlocked. This means the node running this SW assumes that the peer is no more capable of processing 802.1as frames. The node running the SW then waits till it successful calculates the path delay (which essentially means the peer is again capable of processing 802.1as frames.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a kag/asa 08/25/10 First release 3.00a asa 04/10/12 Disabled enabling of promiscuous mode. This is required for AxiEthernet cores with version v3_01_a onwards because of a change in the AVB implementation. 4.0 asa 03/06/14 Fix for CR 740863. Added a #warning message for users of this example to take note of the fact that we have just used a typical value for XAVB_CLOCK_LOCK_THRESHOLD and users may want to change it as per their requirements. 5.17 ml 11/15/23 Fix compilation errors reported with -std=c2x compiler flag
